misconceptionSummary: Students use the phrase linear sequence without understanding that it means equal first differences.

visuals.ruleText: A linear sequence has a constant difference
visuals.subText: Each step changes by the same amount

steps.narration: The defining feature of a linear sequence is a constant difference.
One sequence is linear because the change is constant. The other is not.

lines.text: Constant means unchanged from step to step

steps.narration: Constant means the size of the step does not change.

lines.text: If the difference is constant, the pattern can be continued reliably

steps.narration: That is why checking the difference matters so much.

visuals.expression: Linear sequence → same difference every time
visuals.label: Equal first differences define the pattern

steps.narration: A sequence is linear because the first differences stay equal.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: thinking a sequence is linear just because it increases. It must increase or decrease by the same amount each time.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Not every rising sequence is linear. The size of the change must stay constant.
